title: oedera steyniae
intro: Oedera steyniae, also known as Cape daphne, is a shrubby, succulent plant native to the Western Cape region of South Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant's extracts have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, Oedera steyniae is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from respiratory issues and other health concerns.
